data.title: White Tech Gains MiCA Authorization in Croatia
data.description: White Tech has been granted authorization by Croatia's HANFA to function as a crypto-asset service provider under the European Union's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation. According to NS3.AI, this approval enables White Tech to provide exchange, transfer, custody, and administration services within the EU's unified crypto framework. White Tech is among the pioneering companies in Croatia to receive MiCA authorization, marking a significant step in the region's crypto regulatory landscape.

data.title: Alberta Investment Management Corp Acquires MicroStrategy Shares
data.description: Alberta Investment Management Corp, a major Canadian institutional asset manager, has disclosed its initial purchase of 1.38 million shares of MicroStrategy (MSTR), valued at $219 million. According to Odaily, this marks the institution's first allocation into Bitcoin-related assets.

data.title: Asian Currencies Face Increased Bearish Bets Amid Oil Price Volatility
data.description: On April 30, Jin10 reported that a Reuters survey indicates increased bearish bets on the Indian rupee, Indonesian rupiah, and Philippine peso due to oil price volatility, which has heightened market concerns over inflation, external balances, and economic growth. According to Jin10, the bi-weekly survey of 10 analysts, forex strategists, and economists shows that bearish bets on the Indonesian rupiah have reached their highest level since October 2022, with similar increases in bearish positions on the Thai baht, Philippine peso, and Indian rupee. Analysts from Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group noted in a report that the Philippine peso and Thai baht remain vulnerable due to their strong correlation with oil prices, while Indonesia's unfavorable fiscal and energy dynamics continue to weaken the rupiah. More credible signals of conflict de-escalation are needed to alleviate depreciation pressures. Meanwhile, participants maintained long positions on the Singapore dollar and Malaysian ringgit and reduced bearish bets on the South Korean won.

data.title: Binance to Renew USDC Simple Earn Flexible Products Campaign
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is set to renew its campaign for USDC Simple Earn Flexible Products. During the promotion period from 2026-05-01 00:00:00 (UTC) to 2026-05-31 23:59:59 (UTC), users subscribing to USDC Flexible Products can earn up to 5.6% APR. This includes an exclusive Bonus Tiered APR in addition to Real-Time APR rewards. The subscription process will follow a first-come, first-served basis, with rewards distributed daily to users' Spot Accounts. The Real-Time APR will accrue and accumulate in users' Earn Accounts every minute.

The offered products include USDC Flexible Products with a maximum subscription limit of 300,000,000 USDC per user. The APR during the promotion period is structured as follows: for subscription amounts up to 200 USDC, the APR is 5.6%, which includes a 5% Bonus Tiered APR and approximately 0.6% Real-Time APR. For amounts exceeding 200 USDC, the Real-Time APR remains at approximately 0.6%. Users can start by purchasing USDC through various payment methods or depositing USDC into their Binance account. They can then subscribe to the USDC Simple Earn Flexible Products to begin earning rewards.

Terms and conditions apply, including the requirement for users to complete identity verification during the promotion period to qualify for rewards. Only master accounts are eligible, and sub-accounts are excluded. Changes to the Binance Simple Earn Rewards Rate will be published on the platform as needed. Users are advised to refer to the Binance Simple Earn Terms & Conditions and Risk Warning for more information. The rewards are distributed from Binance's own funds, based on market conditions. Redemption requests may be delayed if there is a high volume, and Binance reserves the right to amend the terms and conditions of the promotion at its discretion.

data.title: Italy's March Unemployment Rate Lower Than Expected
data.description: Italy's unemployment rate for March was reported at 5.2%, according to Jin10. This figure is slightly below the anticipated rate of 5.3%. The previous value was revised from 5.30% to 5.4%.

data.title: Wasabi Protocol Suffers Security Breach Resulting in Significant Losses
data.description: According to CertiK monitoring, Wasabi Protocol has been targeted in a security breach, resulting in the loss of approximately $2.9 million. According to Foresight News, the incident highlights ongoing vulnerabilities within the protocol, raising concerns about the security measures in place to protect digital assets. The breach underscores the importance of robust security frameworks in the rapidly evolving digital finance landscape.

data.title: Alberta Investment Management Corp. makes its first purchase of $219 million from Strategy MSTR.
data.description: An article published on the X platform stated that Alberta Investment Management Corp., a Canadian government-owned company, disclosed its first purchase of 1.38 million shares of Strategy (MSTR), valued at $219 million. This marks the first time the firm, one of Canada's largest institutional asset managers, has allocated assets related to Bitcoin. (BitcoinTreasuries.NET)

data.title: Global Smartphone Shipments Reach 298.5 Million Units in Q1 2026
data.description: Global smartphone shipments reached 298.5 million units in the first quarter of 2026, marking a 1% year-on-year increase, according to Jin10. Omdia's latest data indicates that leading manufacturers like Samsung and Apple have proactively increased channel distribution in anticipation of rising memory and component costs. This strategy has resulted in better-than-expected overall sales performance for the industry. The imbalance between supply and demand may lead to more significant market adjustments in the second quarter and the latter half of 2026.

data.title: Hyperliquid Oil Short Positions Face Losses Amid Rising Funding Fees
data.description: An analyst @ai_9684xtpa posted on X that an oil short seller on Hyperliquid is incurring losses while paying funding fees. The address 0x60a…685f6 recently added $1.2 million in USDC as margin to increase the liquidation price of their oil short positions. Currently, the trader holds short positions of 103,000 $CL and 21,000 $BRENTOIL, with a total value of $13.58 million and an unrealized loss of $1.8 million. The latest liquidation prices are $138.48 and $144.64, respectively, with funding fees reaching $240,000.

data.title: A Hyperliquid address holds a $13.58 million short position in crude oil, with a paper loss of $1.8 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, address (0x60a...685f6) deposited an additional 1.2 million USDC margin into Hyperliquid ten minutes ago to increase the liquidation price of its short crude oil positions. Currently, this address holds short positions in 103,000 CL and 21,000 BRENTOIL, with a total value of $13.58 million and a floating loss of $1.8 million. The latest liquidation prices are $138.48 and $144.64 respectively, and funding fees have reached $240,000.

data.title: STOCKS | Qualcomm Shares Rise 12.5% Pre-Market as CEO Predicts Smartphone Market Rebound
data.description: Qualcomm shares rose by 12.5% in pre-market trading. According to Jin10, the increase follows a statement from the company's CEO, who anticipates a rebound in the smartphone market after the third fiscal quarter.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Close Lower as Hang Seng Index Falls 1.28%
data.description: Hong Kong's stock market closed with losses as the Hang Seng Index dropped by 1.28% and the Hang Seng Tech Index declined by 0.79%. According to Jin10, chip stocks experienced volatility but ultimately moved higher, while the commercial aerospace sector saw gains in the afternoon. Meanwhile, sectors such as power equipment, home appliances, and oil showed strength. In contrast, battery manufacturers, new energy vehicle companies, printed circuit board (PCB) producers, and non-ferrous metals were among the sectors with the largest declines.

data.title: Major Banks and Financial Institutions Release Economic Growth Estimates
data.description:
According to Jin10, several major banks and financial institutions have released their economic growth estimates. UOB predicts a decrease of 0.2%, while RBC and Standard Chartered both forecast an increase of 1.3%. Dekabank expects a growth of 1.6%, and Lloyds Bank anticipates a rise of 1.8%.

Nomura Securities, Commerzbank, and Moody's Analytics all project a 2.0% growth. ANZ and Stifel Investment Bank forecast a 2.2% increase, as do Pantheon Macroeconomics, S&P Ratings, TD Securities, UBS, and BMO.

ABN AMRO, Bank of America, Mizuho Securities, UniCredit, and Morgan Stanley all estimate a 2.4% growth. Julius Baer and JPMorgan Chase predict a 2.5% increase, while Capital Economics and Citigroup expect a 2.6% rise. Barclays also forecasts a 2.6% growth.

HSBC Holdings and ING project a 2.7% increase, with Wells Fargo and BNP Paribas estimating a 2.8% growth. Deutsche Bank also anticipates a 2.8% rise.

Scotiabank predicts a 3.0% growth, Goldman Sachs expects a 3.3% increase, and Société Générale forecasts a 3.9% rise. The previous data showed a growth of 0.5%, while Reuters had expected a 2.3% increase.

data.title: Oil Prices Surge Amid Global Market Volatility
data.description: Oil prices have experienced a significant increase, contributing to heightened volatility in global markets. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that this surge in oil prices is influencing various sectors, including technology futures, which have shown a slight upward trend. The fluctuations in the market are attributed to ongoing geopolitical tensions and economic uncertainties affecting investor sentiment. Analysts are closely monitoring these developments as they could have broader implications for global trade and economic stability. The rise in oil prices is seen as a key factor driving market dynamics, with potential impacts on inflation and consumer spending. As the situation evolves, market participants are advised to stay informed and consider the potential risks and opportunities presented by these changes.

data.title: The probability of Polymarket's "Printr public offering raising over $3 million" has fallen to 15%, a drop of over 80% within the day.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the probability of Printr raising over $3 million in its public sale on Polymarket has dropped to 15%, a decrease of over 80% within the day, with a trading volume of nearly $4.4 million. This market settles its claims based on whether the total subscription amount raised by Printr on the Sonar platform exceeds a set threshold; if it does, it's considered "yes," otherwise "no." The settlement data comes from Printr's official fundraising page. If the final subscription amount cannot be confirmed by May 31, 2026, or if relevant information is unavailable, the market will be considered "no." Furthermore, if the fundraising period is extended, the subscription amount during the extended period will also be included in the statistics. Previous news: Printr's founder announced a full refund of community fundraising funds. Odaily Seer continues to monitor prediction markets, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Germany's Q1 GDP Growth Matches Previous Quarter
data.description: Germany's seasonally adjusted GDP for the first quarter showed a growth rate of 0.3%, according to initial estimates. According to Jin10, this figure aligns with the previous quarter's growth rate and slightly surpasses the anticipated 0.20%.

data.title: USD/JPY Experiences Sharp Decline
data.description: The USD/JPY currency pair experienced a significant short-term drop, falling by 80 points and briefly dipping below the 160 mark, reaching a low of 159.79. According to ChainCatcher, this movement reflects ongoing volatility in the foreign exchange market.

data.title: Tether issues 1 billion USDT on the Tron network.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Tether has issued 1 billion USDT on the Tron network.

data.title: Gate US will host a high-end closed-door reception during Consensus Miami, focusing on institutional-grade crypto infrastructure.
data.description: Gate US, in partnership with leading blockchain security firm Halborn, will host a VIP Institutional Soirée during Consensus Miami on May 5th. The event aims to build a high-quality platform for institutional collaboration and networking. Located at the prestigious Villa Casa Casuarina (the Versace mansion), the event will feature in-depth discussions on topics such as crypto industry trends, security infrastructure development, and compliance prospects. This invitation-only event is expected to attract over 200 key participants from institutional investors, funds, family offices, and fintech companies, focusing on practical business exchanges such as capital deployment and trade execution optimization. Beyond industry conferences, Gate US is continuously strengthening its connectivity and service capabilities in the institutional market by creating more targeted communication opportunities. It is understood that Gate US has successfully obtained a Money Transmitter License in Idaho, USA. Currently, Gate US holds a total of 35 state-level licenses, and its compliance business footprint has further expanded to 46 U.S. jurisdictions, including Illinois, Ohio, Michigan, North Carolina, Georgia, Arizona, and Wisconsin.

data.title: Spot gold touched $4,600 per ounce, up 1.25% on the day.
data.description: Gate data shows that spot gold touched $4,600 per ounce, up 1.25% on the day.
